0.vision: A mission-critical, zivilisationsweites (civilization-scale) infrastructure combining deterministic SECP256k1 cryptographic key derivation based on the 'Linke-Principle' with high-frequency hardware propulsion control and algebraic state inversion.
0.architecture: A distributed, polyglot microservice ecosystem utilizing a Rust-based Real-Time Operating System (LoomOS) core for nanosecond-precision bus communication (LoomBus) and a Python-Rust hybrid for high-precision modular arithmetic. The system employs a Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters) design to isolate BigInt arithmetic from gRPC/REST delivery, a Sidecar pattern for HSM-backed cryptographic operations, and a Capability-Object Security Model (POLA) for hardware access control. Audit logging is handled via an immutable WORM-based ledger with SHA-256 hash-chaining for integrity verification.

techStack.frontend: React 18 with WebGL for 3D Moire-Interference visualization and WebAssembly (WASM) for client-side Zero-Knowledge (ZK) proofs and SecureVault memory isolation.
techStack.backend: Python 3.12 (FastAPI) with MyPy for business logic and Rust 1.75 (num-bigint, tokio, crossbeam) for FFI-based modular arithmetics and the LoomBus real-time kernel.
techStack.database: PostgreSQL 16 with TimescaleDB for high-density telemetry; Redis for distributed sliding-window rate-limiting and metadata caching; AWS CloudHSM/Azure Dedicated HSM for root seed entropy protection.
techStack.infra: Docker-orchestrated Kubernetes (EKS/AKS) using PodAntiAffinity, FPGA-integrated Edge Nodes (Xilinx Artix-7) for GaN-Gate timing, and Terraform-managed global Anycast IP distribution.

0.databaseSchema: Table 'DerivationProfiles' { profile_id: UUID, curve_name: String, scaling_n: BigInt, geometry_g: BigInt, entropy_h: BigInt }; Table 'Telemetry' { time: TIMESTAMPTZ, device_id: UUID, flux_vector: VECTOR(3), gate_temp: FLOAT, voltage: FLOAT }; Table 'CapabilityTokens' { token_id: UUID, bitmask: BIGINT, expiration: TIMESTAMP, owner_id: UUID }; Table 'AuditChain' { audit_id: UUID, timestamp: TIMESTAMP, action: String, parameter_hash: String, hmac_link: String };
apiEndpoints: POST /api/v1/derive-key - Core Linke-Principle key derivation (r, s, z, n, g, h)
POST /api/v1/chronoplast/invert - Algebraic inversion of cryptographic states
POST /api/v1/drive/force - Real-time propulsion target for GaN-amplifiers
GET /api/v1/bus/status - LoomBus health and SPSC queue depth monitoring
STREAM /ws/v1/telemetry - Low-latency WebSocket for 3D Moire flux visualization
GET /api/v1/audit/verify - Integrity check of the SHA-256 log chain
0.securityStrategy: Implementation of constant-time modular inversion to mitigate side-channel attacks. Access is governed by bit-masked 64-bit Capability Tokens (POLA). All private scalars (k, d) are restricted to volatile memory (MLOCK) and wiped post-computation. Communication is strictly over TLS 1.3 with mandatory mTLS for inter-service gRPC and HMAC-based request signing for hardware-bound propulsion commands.
0.scalingPlan: Horizontal Pod Autoscaling (HPA) based on 'zk_generation_queue_depth' and CPU saturation. Multi-region Anycast deployment for sub-10ms latency for blockchain nodes. Vertical scaling of propulsion threads using hardware affinity on ARM/RISC-V cores with FPGA-offloaded arithmetic.
priorityFeatures: Deterministic SECP256k1 key recovery via Linke-Constants
Immutable WORM-compliant Audit Trail with SHA-256 Chaining
Algebraic state inversion engine (Chronoplast) for cryptographic reconstruction
Client-side WASM-based ZK-Proof generation for parameter validation

1.folderStructure: loue-root/├── services/│   ├── engine-core-rust/ (LoomOS Kernel)│   │   ├── src/│   │   │   ├── domain/ (Arithmetic, Models)│   │   │   ├── ports/ (Bus, Crypto, Repository)│   │   │   ├── adapters/ (LoomBus-SPSC, HSM-Client, TimescaleDB)│   │   │   └── main.rs│   │   └── Cargo.toml│   ├── logic-api-python/ (FastAPI)│   │   ├── app/│   │   │   ├── core/ (Linke-Principle, Chronoplast)│   │   │   ├── api/ (v1-endpoints)│   │   │   ├── bridge/ (PyO3-Rust-FFI)│   │   │   └── schemas/│   │   └── main.py│   └── sidecar-hsm/ (Rust-HSM-Proxy)├── packages/│   ├── wasm-zk-vault/ (Crates for Frontend)│   └── loom-bus-lib/ (Shared SPSC structures)├── frontend/│   ├── src/│   │   ├── components/ (3D-Moire-Visualizer)│   │   ├── hooks/ (WASM-ZK-Integration)│   │   └── store/ (Capability-Context)│   └── public/├── infra/│   ├── terraform/ (Anycast, EKS, FPGA-Config)│   └── docker/├── proto/ (gRPC/Protobuf Definitions)└── audit-ledger/ (WORM-Logic-SHA256)
designPatterns: WORM (Write Once Read Many) Immutable Ledger
Strategy Pattern for Modular Arithmetic Engines
1.stateManagement: Distributed algebraic state tracking utilizing Chronoplast inversion logic; UI state managed via React Context and WebGL BufferGeometry; Private scalar state restricted to MLOCK-guarded volatile memory with secure-wipe on drop.
1.cachingStrategy: Redis Cluster for distributed sliding-window metadata and rate-limiting; Local L1 LRU cache for high-frequency Linke-Constants; AWS CloudHSM internal key-wrapping cache for root entropy protection.
1.rateLimiting: Bit-masked 64-bit Capability Token validation at the LoomBus ingress; Redis-backed Leaky Bucket algorithm for public API endpoints; hardware-level throttle on GaN-amplifier bus commands.
1.errorBoundaryStrategy: Rust-level Panic-to-Result propagation with supervision trees; Python middleware for global exception mapping; React Error Boundaries for Moire-Interference visualization isolation; Fail-safe hardware state-lockdown on HSM communication timeout.

frontendComponents: MoireVisualizer: A Three.js-based WebGL component for 4D algebraic state inversion visualization.
ZKCapabilityProvider: A React Context wrapper managing the WASM-ZK-Vault instance for client-side signing.
LoomBusStream: A hook-based event listener for real-time SPSC feedback from the Rust core.
AuditLedgerTable: A read-only UI for the WORM-logic SHA256 immutable history.
GaNThrottleMonitor: A hardware-latency dashboard for real-time bus performance tracking.
2.authImplementation: Implementation follows the Capability-Object Security Model (POLA). Users authenticate via OIDC to receive a hardware-wrapped 64-bit Capability Token. This token is bit-masked at the LoomBus ingress; only tokens with valid permissions for specific MLOCK-guarded memory addresses can trigger state transitions. Private keys never leave the sidecar-hsm, providing high-entropy root protection via key-wrapping.
2.models:
class CapabilityToken(BaseModel): 
    token_id: UUID4 
    bitmask: int 
    expiry: datetime 
class ChronoplastState(BaseModel): 
    scalar_v: bytes 
    algebraic_index: int 
    inversion_proof: str 
class AuditEntry(BaseModel): 
    tx_hash: str 
    payload: dict 
    timestamp: float
2.loggingMiddleware: Structured JSON logging using Python's 'structlog' and Rust's 'tracing-subscriber'. Middleware intercepts every FastAPI request, generating a unique Correlation-ID that is passed through the PyO3 bridge to the Rust SPSC bus. Logs are synchronously mirrored to the WORM audit-ledger where each entry is hashed with the previous state's SHA256 to ensure tamper-evidence.

3.injectionPrevention: Implement strict Serde-based deserialization for the AlgebraicOp buffer within the Rust LoomKernel to prevent buffer overflows or malformed instruction injection. Use Pydantic's 'validate_assignment' and 'Strict' types for CapabilityToken and ChronoplastState to ensure bitmask and scalar_v integrity before the data reaches the PyO3 bridge.
3.cspPolicy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; connect-src 'self' http://sidecar-hsm:8080; worker-src 'self' blob:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none';
3.csrfImplementation: Deploy a Double-Submit Cookie pattern reinforced by the 'X-Capability-Proof' header. Configure session cookies with 'SameSite=Strict', 'Secure', and 'HttpOnly' flags. The mandatory ZK-proof generation for every request serves as a cryptographically signed non-predictable token that mitigates cross-site state-changing execution.
3.advancedRateLimit: Implement a sliding-window rate limiter at the API Gateway level (Nginx/Envoy) set to 100 requests per 10 seconds per unique Capability Token. Integrate with the GaNThrottleMonitor to trigger 429 Too Many Requests or inject artificial latency if the SPSC bus depth exceeds 80% to prevent exhaustion attacks on the HSM.
3.encryptionStandard: In-transit: TLS 1.3 with ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384. At-rest: AES-256-GCM with keys stored in the HSM-sidecar using KMIP. All MLOCK-guarded memory segments must utilize the 'Zeroize' trait in Rust to ensure sensitive algebraic states are wiped from RAM immediately after the sign_state operation.
securityHeaders: Permissions-Policy: accelerometer=(), camera=(), geolocation=(), microphone=(), payment=()
3.sanitizationLogic: Enforce a byte-level schema whitelist for the 'op' input in the LoomKernel, rejecting any sequence not mapping to defined AlgebraicOp variants. Use the bitmask to perform a bitwise AND check against the targeted MLOCK memory addresses; if the result is zero, the request is dropped before being pushed to the SPSC bus.
3.auditLogDesign: Implement a cryptographic hash-chaining mechanism where each entry contains a Blake3 hash of the previous record. Logs are streamed to a WORM (Write Once Read Many) storage with S3 Object Lock enabled. Every log entry must include the OIDC Correlation-ID and the bitmask-derived Capability ID to ensure full non-repudiation of state transitions.

4.dockerConfig: Multi-stage build: Stage 1 (Build) uses rust:1.75 and python:3.11-slim to compile LoomKernel via PyO3. Stage 2 (Runtime) uses debian:bookworm-slim. Installs libssl3 and ca-certificates. Sets CAP_IPC_LOCK to allow MLOCK-guarded memory segments. User defined as non-root (UID 10001). Entrypoint executes the Python/Rust hybrid bridge with jemalloc for optimized memory management.
4.k8sManifests: Deployment: 3 replicas with 'topologySpreadConstraints' for HA. Sidecar: 'hsm-sidecar' container on localhost:8080. SecurityContext: 'allowPrivilegeEscalation: false', 'capabilities: add: ["IPC_LOCK"]'. Service: Type: ClusterIP for internal, LoadBalancer (NLB) for ingress with TLS 1.3 termination. NetworkPolicy: Restricted to ingress-controller and HSM-sidecar traffic only.
4.ciCdPipeline: GitHub Actions: 1. Rust-clippy and Cargo-test (AlgebraicOp validation). 2. Pydantic-check and Pytest. 3. Snyk/Trivy container scanning. 4. Kaniko image build to ECR. 5. Helm upgrade to EKS using OIDC-based IAM roles. 6. Post-deploy integration test verifying ZK-proof generation and 'X-Capability-Proof' header enforcement.
4.terraformPlan: Provider: AWS. Resources: 'aws_eks_cluster' with encrypted secrets. 'aws_s3_bucket' for audit logs with 'object_lock_enabled = true' and 'mfa_delete = true'. 'aws_kms_key' for AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption. 'aws_security_group' allowing only 443 from CloudFront. 'aws_iam_role' with IRSA for KMIP sidecar access.
4.monitoringStack: Prometheus: Custom metrics for 'SPSC_bus_depth' and 'GaNThrottle_latency'. Grafana: Dashboards for tracking ZK-proof verification success vs. failure. Alertmanager: High-priority alerts if HSM latency exceeds 200ms or if 'Zeroize' operations fail in memory segments. Loki: Aggregating hash-chained audit logs for non-repudiation analysis.
4.autoScalingRules: HPA: Scaling based on custom metric 'gan_throttle_bus_pressure'. Scale-up threshold: bus_depth > 65% for 2 minutes. Scale-down: bus_depth < 20% for 10 minutes. Node-level: AWS Karma/Cluster Autoscaler targeting m5.large instances with Nitro Enclaves for enhanced isolation.
4.backupStrategy: Audit Logs: S3 Cross-Region Replication to a separate 'Security-Audit' AWS account. Database: Daily snapshots of state metadata via RDS/Aurora with 35-day retention. Key Management: HSM-backed keys utilizing KMIP with off-site backup in a FIPS 140-2 Level 3 compliant facility.

5.unitTestSuite: Cargo-test suite for LoomKernel AlgebraicOp validation; Pytest for Pydantic schema integrity; Rust-clippy for memory safety audits; PyO3 bridge serialization testing for zero-copy data transfer between Python 3.11 and Rust 1.75.
5.integrationTests: HSM sidecar connectivity validation on localhost:8080; SPSC bus saturation mock testing; X-Capability-Proof header enforcement via mock ingress; KMIP protocol handshake validation for Nitro Enclave isolation; Zeroize memory segment verification after MLOCK release.
e2eScenarios: End-to-end ZK-proof generation flow: Client request triggers Rust kernel via PyO3, verifies proof, signs via HSM sidecar, and archives hash-chained logs to S3 with Object Lock.
Resilience and Scaling: Induce artificial GaNThrottle_latency to exceed 200ms, triggering HPA scale-up based on gan_throttle_bus_pressure while verifying multi-AZ topology spread constraints.
5.loadTestingParams: Target: 5,000 req/s with 15k concurrent VUs; Thresholds: p99 latency < 200ms for HSM operations, SPSC_bus_depth < 85%; Duration: 4-hour soak test to monitor jemalloc heap fragmentation and MLOCK memory stability.
5.perfOptimizations: Configure jemalloc with background_thread:true; optimize PyO3 GIL acquisition frequency; pin Rust worker threads to Nitro Enclave vCPUs; leverage CAP_IPC_LOCK to prevent swap-out of sensitive ZK-proof witness data.
5.dbOptimization: Implement hash-indexed partitioning for state metadata in RDS/Aurora; utilize S3 Select for rapid retrieval of cold audit logs; optimize KMIP key-cache TTL to reduce round-trips to the HSM.
5.bundleOptimization: Multi-stage Docker pruning to exclude Rust/Build-essential toolchains from the final 80MB runtime image; stripping symbols from LoomKernel binary; utilizing debian:bookworm-slim to minimize layer overhead and CVE surface area.
5.latencyImprovements: Enable TLS 1.3 session resumption; implement lock-free SPSC (Single Producer Single Consumer) bus for inter-stage communication; tune Nitro Enclave vSock buffer sizes; utilize Nitro-accelerated AES-256-GCM hardware instructions.

6.apiDocumentation: OpenAPI 3.1 specification exposing /v1/zk-proof/generate, /v1/audit/logs, and /v1/hsm/status. Includes X-Capability-Proof header requirements for Nitro Enclave isolation and HSM-signed payload schemas for cryptographic integrity.
adminDashboardFeatures: Real-time SPSC bus saturation and GaNThrottle latency monitoring
Nitro Enclave vCPU and memory allocation visualizer
HSM Sidecar connectivity and KMIP handshake health status
Hash-chained audit log explorer with S3 Select integration
Jemalloc heap fragmentation and MLOCK memory stability metrics
6.analyticsStrategy: Integration with Prometheus for real-time gan_throttle_bus_pressure metrics and Grafana for p99 latency tracking. Long-term storage of ZK-proof throughput and HSM utilization patterns via hash-indexed partitioning in RDS Aurora for business intelligence.
6.featureFlags: Dynamic toggles for GaNThrottle_latency thresholds, KMIP key-cache TTL adjustments, and selective enablement of ZK-proof witness data zeroization for debug environments.
6.multiTenancy: Logical isolation enforced via X-Capability-Proof headers and hash-indexed RDS partitioning. Tenant-specific cryptographic isolation is maintained through distinct HSM key aliases and Nitro Enclave vSock buffer segmentation.
6.whiteLabeling: API-first architecture supports tenant-branded SDK wrappers and customizable hash-chained log headers, allowing downstream providers to offer ZK-proof services under proprietary identities.
6.operationsRunbook: Standard procedures for Nitro Enclave lifecycle management, jemalloc background thread tuning, S3 Object Lock configuration for compliance, and automated HPA scale-up response to bus pressure alerts.
6.troubleshootingGuide: Diagnostic workflows for resolving GaNThrottle latency spikes, PyO3 GIL contention issues, HSM sidecar handshake failures, and Nitro-accelerated AES-256-GCM hardware instruction mismatches.
6.executiveSummary: A high-performance, enterprise-grade ZK-proof processing engine (LoomKernel) featuring a hybrid Rust-Python architecture. The system leverages Nitro Enclaves for secure execution, HSM-backed signing for integrity, and a lock-free SPSC bus for sub-200ms p99 latency. Fully optimized for memory safety and zero-copy data transfer, it provides a scalable, audit-ready infrastructure for complex cryptographic workflows.
